nodes.label: Amar School Management — Web Rebuild PRD
Amar School Management — Web Rebuild Architecture
Figma mockup: Design System style reference sheet
Figma mockup: Single shared login screen
Figma mockup: Super Admin Dealer detail / Territory assignments
Amar School LMS MVP Build Handoff Doc
Family Design System (standalone Figma community bundle export)
nodes.rationale: Proprietary Figma-community bundler export (JS-rendered thumbnail shell, ~125k tokens of bundled JS); not directly parseable as static markup. Cited by ADR 0006 as the source design system whose tokens (color palette, radii, shadows, Plus Jakarta Sans + Hind Siliguri) were ported into ui/shared/design-system.css and later the Tailwind @theme block.

nodes.label: ADR 0001: Dual-path ingest for attendance-machine sync
nodes.rationale: A cloud web app can't reach attendance hardware behind a school's router. Rather than requiring every school to own push-capable (ADMS-style) hardware, support two ingest paths into the same per-School attendance API endpoint: (1) devices that push logs directly, and (2) a local bridge agent that polls LAN devices for schools whose hardware can't push. Avoids forcing hardware upgrades while not building a permanent on-site-agent dependency for schools with newer equipment.

nodes.label: ADR 0002: Shared database, shared schema, tenant_id-scoped multi-tenancy
nodes.rationale: Legacy already runs every School through one shared MySQL DB/schema distinguished by a company/school ID column. Kept that shape in Postgres: one DB, one schema, school_id column per School-scoped table, enforced by Postgres RLS plus app-layer checks. Rejected schema-per-tenant and database-per-tenant: they multiply migration/operational complexity and conflict with the free-tier/prototype-first hosting approach (single small Postgres instance, not N databases).

nodes.label: ADR 0003: One application, one login, role-based views
nodes.rationale: Legacy ships as three separate desktop apps/logins (AmarSchoolManagement for Schools, SuperAdminSchoolManagement for vendor Super Admin/Dealers/Gov Officials). Merged into a single Next.js app with one login page; what a user sees is determined by role. Chosen because all audiences share one backend/DB (ADR 0002) and the user explicitly wanted one shared login. Trade-off: School, Dealer, and Super Admin release cycles are now coupled, and route-level access control must be enforced carefully per role.

nodes.label: ADR 0004: Bangla-primary bilingual UI, not legacy's English-only chrome
nodes.rationale: Legacy Java Swing app has zero Bangla in its interface (verified against source). The rebuild deliberately deviates: UI ships bilingual with Bangla as default/primary and English as switchable secondary. Rejected staying English-only (cheapest, matches legacy) and English-primary-with-Bangla-secondary (common B2B default) because the product serves Bangladeshi school staff day-to-day, for whom Bangla is more natural. This is real added scope over legacy: full i18n infrastructure and translated copy across every screen.

nodes.label: ADR 0006: Tailwind for the production app, not the mockups' design-system.css
nodes.rationale: The ui/ mockups ship a hand-rolled design-system.css themed after the Family Design System. Rather than importing it wholesale into the production Next.js app, the app is styled with Tailwind CSS v4: Family design tokens declared once in the Tailwind @theme block, mockup visual vocabulary (app shell, tables, KPI cards, badges, pill buttons) rebuilt as shared React components using Tailwind utilities. Consequence: design-system.css remains source of truth for mockups only; a palette change must be applied in both places while mockups are still referenced.

nodes.label: ADR 0007: Browser-native print, no server-side PDF renderer
nodes.rationale: Nearly every PRD module ends in a print-preview -> print/PDF flow. Three facts settled the decision: (1) Bangla needs full complex-text shaping that the browser's own engine (HarfBuzz) does perfectly but @react-pdf/renderer's fontkit shaping is unreliable, and headless Chromium would ship a ~50MB browser into a serverless function; (2) no PRD flow needs a PDF *file* — every printable is a browser-print/save preview, nothing is emailed or stored server-side; (3) the MVP fee receipt already prints this way successfully. Decision: printables are server-rendered Tailwind pages using window.print(), with a shared template layer in web/components/print/ (PrintPage, InstituteHeader, InfoGrid, SignatureRow, QrFooterRow, PrintButton) mirroring the legacy C_TAMPLATES shared-header/footer pattern. No PDF dependency added; zero serverless weight.

Bilingual markup rule: every UI-chrome string wrapped in paired data-i18n=bn/en spans, Bangla default — rationale: consistency across all screens, not applied to free-text data content

Brand-purple-is-chrome-only rule: --brand-*/--accent reserved for chrome & primary actions, never used to encode status — rationale: keep status semantics exclusively in badge-success/warning/danger/info
Compact density rule: table.data-table fixed at 36px row height, no spacious/density-toggle variant
Shared app-shell markup pattern (sidebar nav + topbar lang/theme switches + content) copied verbatim across School-product pages
List-screen pattern: toolbar (search+filters+create) + table.data-table + status via badge-*; create/edit screens use form-grid; destructive actions use a modal confirm
Closed-exam-is-permanent rule: once an exam is closed it stays closed for every user via normal access — no reopen path for anyone; a systemic decision, not a permission-gated restriction
Extended-access pattern: an actor (Dealer/Gov Official) can be granted access to a School/Institute individually, outside their normal territory/jurisdiction, shown via badge-extended
Print-preview framing pattern: bordered 'page' surface with institute header, info rows, signature/photo blocks, and a powered-by footer, used for mark-sheet/progress-report/admit-card previews
Staff User allow-list model: nav items not granted by School Owner render as nav-item disabled; reaching a denied screen shows a plain permission-denied message state
Gallery Album Detail Page — Annual Sports Day 2026 (school-owner/gallery-album-detail.html)
One Fee Record Per Student Per Month Rule
Behaviour Log Auto-Lock After 3 Days Rule
Working Days Formula (Total minus Off-days minus Approved Leave minus Present)
Absence SMS Daily Batch Evaluation Rule
Cumulative (running-total) fee record model
nodes.rationale: Fee records store only running/cumulative totals (pay_amount, fine, adjust_amount/discount, due_amount) per student per period; there is no underlying list of individual past payments kept per record.

nodes.label: Binary (all-or-nothing) staff module access model
nodes.rationale: The staff permissions list is the single source of truth: each screen/module is either fully accessible or fully inaccessible for a staff login. There is no separate view/edit/delete sub-permission granularity.

nodes.label: Publish blocked until scheduling conflict resolved
nodes.rationale: Both seat-plan generation (roll-range overlap between rooms) and class-routine building (teacher/room double-booking) disable the Publish action and surface a red conflict badge until the underlying overlap is resolved.

nodes.label: School Detail Admin Page (Adarsha High School)
Government Official Detail Page (Md. Aminul Islam)
Cluster (named grouping of Schools within a location node)
Tier is per-assignment, not per-entity
nodes.rationale: Tier is a property of each individual territory assignment, not one value on the Dealer/Official as a whole — the same Dealer or Official can hold different tiers across different assignments. Tier is descriptive/organizational only; it does not affect pricing, commission, or permissions. The 'Extended access' row is an assignment pointing at a single out-of-territory/out-of-jurisdiction School rather than a location node, so it is visually flagged rather than blended in silently, for accountability.

nodes.label: Free (Tk 0) subscription code is a normal, valid option
nodes.rationale: Setting price to 0 when generating a batch is a normal, valid option — it produces a free/promotional batch, not a special/hidden case. Redeeming a free code still counts as code history and permanently ends Trial status the same as a paid code. Because a redeemed code (free or paid) can never be deleted, its Delete action is always shown disabled.

nodes.label: School Feature Flags (flat boolean list, no plan-tier grouping)
ADR 0003 — Optimistic Auth Gate
nodes.rationale: proxy() is only an optimistic routing gate for role route groups: pages and RLS re-verify authorization. It routes on two conditions only — no session redirects to /login, wrong role group redirects to the role's own home — rather than being the sole enforcement point.

nodes.label: Next.js Agent Rules ("This is NOT the Next.js you know")
nodes.rationale: This Next.js version has breaking changes vs. training data; agents must read node_modules/next/dist/docs/ before writing code and heed deprecation notices, to avoid generating code against a stale mental model of Next.js APIs.

nodes.label: ADR 0001: Dual-path attendance ingest
nodes.rationale: The ingest endpoint accepts either a single device push ({card_number, tapped_at}) or a bridge-agent batch ({events:[...]}) so both direct hardware callers and batching bridge agents can use the same endpoint; auth is a per-school ingest token, not a user session, since the caller is hardware.

nodes.label: Issue #10: reconciliation must be batch, not synchronous
nodes.rationale: Attendance reconciliation is scheduled daily by Vercel cron after the school day ends; it must never run synchronously with ingest, so it is implemented as a separate batch job callable with an optional backfill date.

nodes.label: Issue #12: daily absence-SMS job
nodes.rationale: Rule evaluation (who counts as absent long enough to notify) lives in SQL/RPC, while dispatch goes through the swappable SmsGateway abstraction so SMS providers can change without touching route code; the send attempt is recorded first (deduped by a unique constraint) before the message is actually sent.

nodes.label: Issue #27: open a student's photo via signed URL
nodes.rationale: The student-photos storage bucket is private, so the route authenticates the caller, looks up the RLS-scoped student row, mints a short-lived (10 min) signed URL, and redirects to it rather than exposing a public bucket.

nodes.label: Issue #45: open a class's syllabus PDF via signed URL
nodes.rationale: Same private-bucket signed-URL pattern as student-photo: authenticate as a School member, look up the RLS-scoped metadata row, mint a short-lived signed URL, redirect.

nodes.label: Migration 0020: composite FKs enforce cross-school scoping
nodes.rationale: Composite foreign keys added in migration 0020 make a cross-school student/employee id fail at the database level; the RLS-scoped lookup in assignCard already only sees the caller's school, giving a clear application-level error before the DB constraint would even be hit.

nodes.label: RLS as authority; actions validate input only
Atomic transfer via single-transaction RPC to avoid orphaned history rows
SMS already sent; log-insert failure must not surface as retryable error
PRD §5.1: Printable admission/ID templates & Old Students soft-archive
dict
nodes.rationale: Minimal bilingual dictionary — Bangla default, English secondary, keys kept flat and screen-prefixed.

nodes.label: SmsGateway (interface)
nodes.rationale: Provider-agnostic interface — nothing outside this module touches a provider directly.

nodes.label: ADR 0007 — shared printable template pieces
nodes.rationale: Establishes composable print pieces (legacy C_TAMPLATES equivalent); pages pass already-translated strings, pieces stay presentation-only.

nodes.label: public.effective_grace_minutes (migration 0012)
nodes.rationale: SQL function is the authority; effectiveGrace() in TS mirrors it for UI use.

nodes.label: 3-day behaviour entry lock trigger (migration 0011)
nodes.rationale: SQL trigger is the authority; isEntryLocked() mirrors it for UI display only.

nodes.label: reconcile_attendance (migration 0017)
nodes.rationale: SQL job is the authority; employeeStatus() is a legacy status-matrix mirror for UI/preview.

nodes.label: redeem_code / decrease_expiry (migration 0008)
nodes.rationale: Database is the authority for subscription expiry math; subscription.ts is a client-side preview mirror.

Both are one-way/irreversible domain states (Exam Closed has no reopen path; used Subscription Codes cannot be deleted) reflecting a shared 'no retroactive rewriting' design principle
